quality.rationale: 纯张量代数恒等式推导，逻辑链条较短且无具体物理场景，但题干与解答完整且数学结构具高泛化性。

origin.question: (b) Establish the following formulae: $\mathbf{V}((a_1 \cdots a_p)) = \mathbf{V}(a_1 \cdots a_p)$ ; $\mathbf{V}[[a_1 \cdots a_p]] = \mathbf{V}[a_1 \cdots a_p]$ ; $\mathbf{V}(a_1 \cdots [a_\ell a_m] \cdots a_p) = 0$ ; $\mathbf{V}[a_1 \cdots [a_\ell a_m] \cdots a_p] = \mathbf{V}[a_1 \cdots a_\ell a_m \cdots a_p]$ .
origin.answer: $\mathbf{V}_{((a_1 \cdots a_p))} = \mathbf{V}_{(a_1 \cdots a_p)}$, $\mathbf{V}_{[[a_1 \cdots a_p]]} = \mathbf{V}_{[a_1 \cdots a_p]}$, $\mathbf{V}_{(a_1 \cdots [a_\ell a_m] \cdots a_p)} = 0$, $\mathbf{V}_{[a_1 \cdots [a_\ell a_m] \cdots a_p]} = \mathbf{V}_{[a_1 \cdots a_\ell a_m \cdots a_p]}$
origin.solution: (b) If $\mathbf{A}_{\mu \nu \dot s \sigma}$ is a totally antisymmetric tensor, i.e.$$(- 1)^{\pi} \mathrm {A}_{\alpha_{\pi_{1}} \dot s \alpha_{\pi_{p}}} = \mathrm {A}_{\alpha_{1}} \dot s \alpha_{p}$$then$$\mathrm {A}_{[ \alpha_{1} \dot s \alpha_{\mathrm {p}} ]} = \frac {1}{\mathrm {p} !} \sum (- 1)^{\pi} \mathrm {A}_{\pi_{1} \dot s \alpha_{\pi_{\mathrm {p}}}} = \mathrm {A}_{\alpha_{1} \dot s \alpha_{\mathrm {p}}}:$But $\mathbf{A}_{\alpha_1\dot s \alpha_{\mathfrak{p}}}\equiv \mathbf{V}_{[\alpha_1\dot s \alpha_{\mathfrak{p}}]}$ is totally antisymmetric, so$$\mathrm {A} \left[ a_{1} \dot s a_{\mathrm {p}}\right] = \mathrm {V} \left[ \left[ a_{1} \dot s a_{\mathrm {p}}\right]\right] = \mathrm {V} \left[ a_{1} \dot s a_{\mathrm {p}}\right].$The theorem for $\mathbf{V}_{((\alpha_1\dot s \alpha_{\mathfrak{p}}))}$ follows analogously.By symmetry $\mathbf{V}_{(a_1\dot s [a_\ell a_m]\dot s a_p)} = \mathbf{V}_{(a_1\dot s [a_m a_\ell ]\dot s a_p)}$ but by antisymmetry $\mathbf{V}_{(a_1\dot s [a_\ell a_m]\dot s a_1)} = -\mathbf{V}_{(a_1\dot s [a_m a_\ell ]\dot s a_p)}$ . Hence $\mathbf{V}$ must vanish.Antisymmetry on two indices is easily expressed as$$\mathbf {V}_{\alpha_{1} \dot s [ a_{\ell} \alpha_{\mathrm {m}} ] \dot s \alpha_{\mathrm {p}}} = \frac {1}{2} \left[ \mathbf {V}_{\alpha_{1} \dot s a_{\ell} a_{\mathrm {m}} \dot s \alpha_{\mathrm {p}}} - \mathbf {V}_{\alpha_{1} \dot s a_{\mathrm {m}} a_{\ell} \dot s \alpha_{\mathrm {p}}}\right] .$Thus$$\mathrm {V}_{[ a_{1} \dot s [ a_{\ell} a_{m} ] \dot s a_{p} ]} = \frac {1}{2 p !} \sum (- 1)^{\pi} \left(\mathrm {V}_{a_{\pi_{1}} \dot s a_{\pi_{m}} a_{\pi_{\ell}} \dot s a_{\pi_{p}}} - \mathrm {V} \dot s a_{\pi_{\ell}} a_{\pi_{m}} \dot s\right).$$But $\mathbf{V}_{\alpha_{\pi_1}\dot s \alpha_{\pi_\ell}\alpha_{\pi_{\mathfrak{m}}}\dot s \alpha_{\pi_{\mathfrak{p}}}} = -\mathbf{V}_{\alpha_{\pi_1}\dot s \alpha_{\pi_{\mathfrak{m}}}\alpha_{\pi_\ell}\dot s \alpha_{\pi_{\mathfrak{p}}}}$ so that$$\mathrm {V} [ a_{1} \dot s [ a_{\ell} a_{m} ] \dot s a_{p} ] = \mathrm {V} [ a_{1} \dot s a_{\ell} a_{m} \dot s a_{p} ].$$
